segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man with a beard and glasses, wearing a dark shirt, intensely examining an X-ray of a spine labeled 'ERECT' and 'R', with visible metal implants. He is in a dimly lit room, surrounded by numerous other X-rays illuminated from below, creating a clinical and focused atmosphere. The camera then cuts to a man in a blue suit over a patterned shirt, speaking seriously in a softly lit room with a staircase in the background. The scene shifts back to the X-rays, now shown in a large, organized grid on a wall, as hands adjust and point to different images, suggesting a collaborative medical analysis. A woman with blonde hair and glasses, wearing a white collared shirt, appears next, speaking with a composed expression in a warm-lit indoor setting. The focus returns to the man with the beard, who continues to scrutinize the X-rays, his tattooed arm visible as he handles the images. The video then cuts to a man with gray hair and round glasses, wearing a light-colored patterned shirt, speaking in a softly lit room with blurred bookshelves behind him, identified as Prof. Ian Harris, an orthopaedic surgeon. The final scene shows the man with the beard lying in bed at night, illuminated by a warm bedside lamp, using his smartphone, his expression weary and contemplative, with a prosthetic leg visible on the nightstand beside him.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a black screen, then transitions into a series of rolling end credits for a production titled 'Pain Factory' by Four Corners. The credits are displayed in white text on a black background, with a large, stylized, golden 3D number '4' occupying the right side of the screen, slowly rotating and illuminated by warm light. The credits list key personnel including reporter Adele Ferguson, producer Chris Gillett, editor Guy Bowden, and various other roles such as camera, sound, and digital lead. As the credits progress, additional contributors are acknowledged, including those from ABC Reference Library, Sydney University, and the State Library of Victoria. The segment also includes social media handles for TikTok accounts related to the story, such as @PRECISIONCAREMEDICAL and @DR.GHATAN. The final frames display the website 'abc.net.au/4corners', the Australian Broadcasting Corporation copyright notice for 2024, and conclude with a static screen featuring the golden '4' logo and the text 'More from FOUR CORNERS' in bold, centered on a black background with subtle golden light flares and animated corner lines, suggesting a transition to more content from the series.
